The implementation now has full survival logic (cells with 2 or 3 neighbors survive) and reproduction logic (dead cells with exactly 3 neighbors become alive).

For the block pattern: [[0,0], [1,0], [0,1], [1,1]] — each cell has exactly 3 neighbors → all survive. Dead neighbors: check e.g. (-1,-1) has 1 neighbor, (-1,0) has 2 neighbors, (2,0) has 2 neighbors, etc. No dead cell has exactly 3 neighbors. So the block should be unchanged.

This test will likely PASS immediately since the implementation is complete.

The function uses sparse representation: cells are [x, y] coordinate pairs. Use toEqual with sorted arrays or expect each element with toContainEqual.

- Therefore the output should be exactly the same 4 cells, and the test should PASS.

